title: gmelina rheedei
intro: Gmelina rheedei, also known as the Indian mahogany or Malabar thorn, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Verbenaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic effects. It is used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Gmelina rheedei include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the affected area.
